title: Add labels to beads
description:
Add arbitrary label support to beads for categorisation (e.g. 'architecture', 'refactor', 'hotfix').

Changes needed:

1. models.py — add 'labels: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)' to the Bead dataclass. Ensure it serialises/deserialises correctly via existing JSON handling.

2. cli.py — add 'takt bead label <bead_id> <label> [<label> ...]' subcommand that appends labels to a bead (idempotent — no duplicates). Add 'takt bead unlabel <bead_id> <label>' to remove a label.

3. cli.py — add '--label <value>' filter to 'takt bead list' (can be specified multiple times; beads must match ALL provided labels).

4. cli.py — show labels in 'takt bead show' output if the bead has any.

5. cli.py — add '--label <label>' option to 'takt bead create' (can be specified multiple times).
acceptance_criteria: takt bead create --label refactor --label hotfix <...> stores both labels on the bead
takt bead label <id> <label> adds the label idempotently
takt bead unlabel <id> <label> removes the label
takt bead list --label refactor returns only beads with that label
takt bead show displays labels when present
Existing beads without labels deserialise without error (empty list default)

handoff_summary.completed:
1. models.py — added `labels: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)` to Bead dataclass and `labels=list(data.get("labels", []))` in `from_dict` for zero-migration backward compat.
2. storage.py — added `labels: list[str] | None = None` parameter to `create_bead`; passed through to Bead constructor.
3. cli.py — added `--label` (repeatable) to `bead create` parser and passed to `storage.create_bead`. Added `--label` / `--label_filter` (repeatable, AND semantics) to `bead list` parser with filtering in `command_bead`. Added `bead label <id> <labels...>` and `bead unlabel <id> <label>` subparsers with idempotent add and safe-remove handlers.
handoff_summary.risks: Low. The only schema change is an additive optional field with a safe default; existing JSON files without a `labels` key deserialise correctly to an empty list.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify all acceptance criteria: create with labels, label/unlabel idempotency, list filtering, show output, and backward compat deserialisation of existing beads.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low — storage.py change adds a new keyword argument with a default; callers that don't pass `labels` continue to work unchanged. No schema migration required.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Labels are stored directly on the Bead dataclass as `list[str]` rather than inside `metadata` dict. This keeps them first-class (filterable, serialisable via `asdict`, visible in show output without special handling) and consistent with how other list fields like `dependencies` and `linked_docs` are modelled. The `--label` filter on `bead list` uses AND semantics (bead must have all specified labels), which is the most useful default for multi-label queries. The `label` subcommand is idempotent — silently skips labels already present rather than erroring, matching the behaviour of similar CLI tools.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) `bead create --label refactor --label hotfix` stores both labels; (2) `bead label <id> existing-label` does not duplicate; (3) `bead unlabel <id> present-label` removes it; (4) `bead unlabel <id> absent-label` exits 0 without error; (5) `bead list --label refactor` returns only matching beads; (6) `bead list --label A --label B` requires both labels (AND); (7) `bead show` JSON includes `labels` key; (8) loading a legacy JSON file without a `labels` key yields an empty list.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Labels are not currently displayed in the `bead list --plain` tabular output — the table uses a fixed column set (`LIST_PLAIN_COLUMNS`). This was not part of the acceptance criteria and can be added as a follow-up if needed.

conflict_risks: Low — storage.py change adds a new keyword argument with a default; callers that don't pass `labels` continue to work unchanged. No schema migration required.

execution_history.summary: Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B-f22201b6-test
Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B-f22201b6-docs

Added arbitrary label support to beads. Labels are stored as `list[str]` on the Bead dataclass with an empty-list default for full backward compatibility. The feature exposes four CLI surfaces: `bead create --label`, `bead label`, `bead unlabel`, and `bead list --label`. Labels appear automatically in `bead show` JSON output via the existing `asdict` serialisation path.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
